articles.author: Aamer Madhani, Sarah El Deeb, Adam Schreck
articles.description: President Donald Trump says he is reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war. He mentioned this on Saturday before boarding Air Force One. Iran reportedly shared a 14-point proposal via Pakistan. Trump rejected a previous proposal this week, but conversations continue, and a three-week ceasefire is holding. Meanwhile, the health of imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi is at high risk, according to her family. The U.S. has warned shipping companies about possible sanctions for paying Iran to pass safely through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has also hanged two men convicted of spying for Israel.
articles.title: Trump says he is reviewing a new Iran proposal to end the war

articles.author: Kirsten Grieshaber, Emma Burrows
articles.description: The German defense minister has responded to the U.S. plan to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, emphasizing the mutual benefits of the U.S. military presence in Europe. Boris Pistorius acknowledged Europe's need to enhance its security within NATO and suggests the U.S. also gains from its deployment. The planned withdrawal over the next six to 12 months reflects tensions in U.S.-Germany relations. President Donald Trump has criticized NATO allies for not supporting his stance against Iran. The Pentagon's decision follows a review of force posture in Europe. NATO has stressed the importance of Europe investing more in defense.
articles.title: Germany's defense minister responds to US troop drawdown plan

articles.author: Stephen Whyno
articles.description: Golden Tempo went from being last in a field of 18 horses in the Kentucky Derby three-quarters of a mile into the race to winning it by a neck. Jose Ortiz saved ground along the rail for much of the trip while long shot Six Speed and other rivals set a hot pace around the track at Churchill Downs. Ortiz began weaving through horses around the far turn but he and Golden Tempo still had 11 horses to pass at the top of the stretch. The jockey who has gotten to know the track and Golden Tempo well charged past everyone to pull off a memorable victory.
articles.title: How did Golden Tempo win the Kentucky Derby?

articles.author: Stephen Whyno
articles.description: Cherie DeVaux's family piled into a big white van and drove from New York to Louisville to watch Golden Tempo run in the Kentucky Derby. The 14-hour drive turned out to be more than worth it when Golden Tempo ran down rivals down the stretch to win. DeVaux became the first woman to train a Derby winner and just the second in any Triple Crown race after Jena Antonucci won with Arcangelo in the 2023 Belmont Stakes. Adrianne DeVaux chronicled the journey on social media with the hashtag #devauxsonthegeaux. They were ready to begin the long journey back Sunday.
articles.title: Cherie DeVaux's family drives together to see her make Kentucky Derby history

articles.author: Gary B. Graves
articles.description: Jockey Jose Ortiz rallied from last to win the Kentucky Derby aboard 23-1 long shot Golden Tempo and complete an impressive weekend sweep. Coming a day after he guided Always a Runner to a Kentucky Oaks victory, Ortiz became the ninth rider to win both of horse racing's marquee events at Churchill Downs. His Derby victory made Cherie DeVaux the first female trainer to win the race and required edging his brother, Irad Ortiz Jr., aboard morning line opening favorite Renegade. Ortiz had five winners on Friday and five seconds on Saturday before winning the Derby.
articles.title: Jockey Jose Ortiz comes from last to pass brother Irad and win Kentucky Derby

articles.author: Abbie Parr, Charlie Riedel, George Walker Iv
articles.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The Kentucky Derby marks its 152nd running, continuing its legacy as America’s longest continuously held sporting event.
articles.title: Photos of the Kentucky Derby horse race

articles.author: Matt Sedensky
articles.description: The U.S. dollar has dropped about 10% against a basket of other major currencies since early 2025. That shift is starting to show up in everyday costs: A weaker dollar means Americans get less when traveling abroad and may pay more for imported goods like food, fuel and electronics. Economists say the effect on prices is usually modest, but it can add up, particularly when stacked on other factors, including inflation, tariffs and a spike in fuel costs due to the Iran war. President Donald Trump has openly supported a weaker dollar, arguing it helps American industry, and some executives agree, saying it's boosted overseas business.
articles.title: A weak dollar can help but also harm

articles.author: Sally Ho
articles.description: The collapse of the U.S.-based Spirit Airlines may mark the end of an era for travelers with a certain financial sensibility. But if you’ve been snagged in their now-defunct flight schedule, here are some things to know on how to get home, and get whole. Look for “rescue fares” and reduced prices on flights from other airlines including American Airlines, United Airlines, Delta Air Lines, JetBlue Airways, Frontier Airlines and Southwest Airlines. Save documentation and request refunds as soon as possible. Employees should be granted travel benefits to get home, and airlines are offering preferential interviews.
articles.title: What to do if you were traveling on Spirit Airlines

articles.description: Spirit Airlines announced it is going out of business after 34 years, saying Saturday it has started winding down operations.
articles.title: Laid off Spirit flight attendant reacts to news of airline shutting down

articles.author: Collin Binkley
articles.description: President Donald Trump is promising to release new UFO records that he says will be “very interesting.” He says the files will be coming “very soon” after he directed the Pentagon to share documents related to UFOs and extraterrestrial life. That directive came even with the Pentagon already years into a process to declassify materials related to UFOs. Congress ordered the effort in 2022. The president's interest has been embraced by a small group of Republicans in Congress who say the Pentagon has failed to provide transparency around mysterious aircraft reported near U.S. military installations.
articles.title: Trump hints at new revelations in UFO files around questions of alien life
